item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would have liked there to be a mention of how many steps there are in the ritual or some method of viewing the document. Without that mentioning "Step 7" feels a bit out of place.

However, I like the idea of performing a satanic ritual for such a trivial purpose such as solving relationship problems. It's so cleverly absurd.

Also, the use of censorship in this article is great. We all can guess what the redacted ingredient is in the process, or what step 7 is, but you never know.

Upvoted.
Also currently under translation to Korean by me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's short, but I really enjoyed it. +1

I also love that an instance of 4747-1 is being kept for observation long-term in Site 56, and I wish I knew more about the instances produced. Does it require food? What does it do while "idle," no humans in the containment chamber for it to attack? I don't know if it's enough for a Tale, but I'd read it. Good work!

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
GOPSTOCK, this comment is rude and unnecessary.

If you don't like an SCP and are not willing to give constructive feedback, just downvote and move on. This comment is entirely unhelpful and comes across as very aggressive. Please do not make comments like this in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short stupid piece of shit. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how barebones this is. I might suggest a redacted or otherwise oblique reference to the actual text (e.g. for details see document yada yada) but it might not need that. Just a taste thing, maybe.

Good job, +1. The recent crop has been a slog of overwritten, undercooked. This was a boone of less is more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is extraordinarily thinly sketched for an idea that provides such fertile material, and what material there is doesn't go in the directions I would find interesting: I want to know what sort of people actually do this, and what typically happens in the aftermath of the ritual, rather than a generic list of spooky ritual ingredients that doesn't add anything and is a textbook case of bad expungement.

This idea had the capacity to be a great exploration of the horrific side of relationship advice culture and the emotional pressure to make bad (or just difficult) relationships work, in the way of Star Signals or A Pickup Artist's Bible skewer self-help and PUA fuckery, but as it is it's more an outline of an idea than a solid article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and sweet. I like it!

One quick qualm: You use the phrase "step 7" in the description of SCP-4747-1, but the list above it is bulleted and is not a sequential set of "steps". Perhaps just a line or so describing how the list has X-number of steps or maybe just removing the words "step 7" would help ease confusion. This is a super short SCP, so make every word count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haven't got what this means, but I do have to admit that it's really well written. I'll give it a reread and think over things then see if I want to change my opinion, but for now I'll remain unvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am incredibly confused and intrigued. Wish there were more to this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very short and it felt like I was missing something at the end, but the sheer "what the fuck" left me really interested into figuring out what was going on and why, to a point where I was (momentarily) thinking about this really heavily. That intrigue is rare to come by, and pulling it off in such a short form is impressive.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is probably the shortest SCP I've ever seen but it works so well
just the right amount of "oh god why"
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this sentiment. The title of the SCP (which did a great job of pulling me in) is almost longer than the article. I don't see enough here to intrigue me. Also, obligatory complaint about unnecessary use of redactions, which I probably would have been fine with if the rest of the article had done anything for me. (I do understand use of redactions for humor or anticlimax, but I have to enjoy the rest of what's going on too.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This whole thing seems to be structured in a way that suggests it's a reference to something, and if that's the case I'm not getting it. And if it isn't, there's just not enough here for me to get anything from. It's just a ritual that summons a monster. It's well enough written, but there's little here to grab me. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Straight forward, simple, concise. I have questions about the SCP-XXXX-1, but they are mostly superficial and would ultimately require you to explain what that redacted 7th ingredient was, and I don't think we need to know.

Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much! More of a side effect than anything else, honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Am I to infer that the entity produced is essentially a drain that absorbs the negative emotions of the individuals in the relationship?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only chose them because they're very image-based mainstream websites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Facebook and Reddit.

Haha, I see you are enlightened, memelord.

Anyways, great stuff, hooked me in till the very end, even if its a bits short

+1

item.title: author post
item.content:encoded:
I work so hard trying to make a few bucks
I pass the hours in a dream
The sweat keeps rolling off the tip of my nose
There's only one thing, one thing, one thing keeps me on my feet

Made for Bright's first challenge. I wrote this in about an hour and a half.
